pair_convention.spot_explanation: EUR/JPY at 183.36 means 1 EUR buys 183.36 Japanese yen. A rising EUR/JPY rate means the euro is strengthening against the yen (more yen required per euro), while a falling rate means the euro is weakening against the yen (fewer yen per euro). The pair is quoted as the number of yen per one euro.

executive_summary: EUR/JPY hit an all-time high of ¥186.87 on 23 January 2026 before retreating to 183.36 as of 11 March 2026 — a decline of approximately 3.5 figures from the cycle peak, with the pair now sitting at the intersection of two powerful and opposing structural forces. Over the past year EUR/JPY has gained approximately 15%, driven by EUR's fiscal renaissance (Germany's €500bn infrastructure fund), JPY's persistent carry-funded weakness, and global risk appetite supporting yen-funded trades. The dominant bilateral narrative has been fractured by the Iran conflict: the yen's safe-haven demand and Japan's acute energy import vulnerability are partially cancelling, while EUR simultaneously suffers from energy-driven external balance deterioration and benefits from fiscal-driven growth repricing. The pair is a moderate-carry vehicle (+125bps EUR over JPY) sitting atop structurally misaligned valuations — EUR/JPY is approximately 41% above OECD PPP fair value of ~¥130 — and operates as a moderate risk-on/risk-off barometer with strong sensitivity to both global equity direction and BoJ normalisation pace. A short, sharp equity correction is probably the most likely route to a lower EUR/JPY in 2026. Our headline view is tactical neutral-to-bearish (conviction 2/5) and strategic bearish (conviction 3/5): the ECB-BoJ rate differential compression path from +125bps toward near-zero over 12-18 months, combined with JPY's mean-reversion potential from the 0th-percentile REER, creates a structural headwind for the pair that the Iran-driven energy shock only partially offsets. The primary caveat is timing: the super central bank week (18-19 March) and Shunto results dominate the tactical horizon and could produce 300-500 pip moves in either direction.

market_context.valuation_assessment: EUR/JPY is structurally expensive by every long-run valuation metric. The EUR dossier places EUR/JPY PPP fair value at approximately ¥130 (EUR/JPY deviation +41%), while the JPY dossier estimates EUR/JPY PPP fair value at approximately ¥123 (using EUR's 33% undervaluation vs JPY). The average of the two dossier estimates implies bilateral PPP fair value of ~¥126-130, making the current 183.36 approximately 41-45% above long-run equilibrium — one of the widest PPP deviations in G10. However, PPP is not actionable on any tactical horizon. The BoJ REER at the all-time low of 67.73 (0th percentile of history back to 1970) and EUR REER at 97.7 (near the 50th percentile) confirm that virtually all of the bilateral valuation gap is attributable to yen undervaluation rather than euro overvaluation. The pair is cheap in JPY terms, near-fairly valued in EUR terms.

market_context.recent_regime: EUR/JPY spent January 2026 pushing through historic highs as German fiscal expansion, manufacturing PMI recovery, and buoyant global risk appetite combined with persistent yen weakness to drive the all-time high above ¥186.87. The regime broke abruptly on 28 February when the Iran conflict triggered simultaneous safe-haven JPY demand (bullish JPY = bearish EUR/JPY) and energy import shock (bearish JPY via terms-of-trade = bullish EUR/JPY). These forces have created an unusual tug-of-war that has reduced the pair's directionality, compressing the net move relative to the individual currency swings. The current regime is elevated vol with muted net pair movement — a hallmark of partially offsetting cross-pressures.

bilateral_carry_analysis.breakeven_move: 125bps annualised × (90/360) = 31.25bps = ~57 pips per quarter. At 10% IV, expected daily move is approximately 115 pips. Quarterly carry accrual (~57 pips) is erased by less than one average daily move — carry provides essentially no buffer against directional risk.
bilateral_carry_analysis.carry_assessment: The EUR/JPY carry differential stands at +125bps (ECB deposit rate 2.00% vs BoJ overnight call rate 0.75%) — the lowest positive carry in the G10 carry matrix for EUR, with EUR earning positive carry only versus JPY (+125bps) and CHF (+200bps). The carry-to-vol ratio of 0.125 is well below the 0.30-0.40 rule-of-thumb threshold for 'carry working,' placing this among the weakest carry-to-vol ratios for any EUR cross. The carry regime is technically positive but economically marginal — a quarterly accrual of only ~57 pips is erased by less than one average daily move of ~115 pips. A regime where the carry-to-vol ratio is below 0.20 historically correlates with elevated risk of carry unwind acceleration. The trajectory is critically bearish for the carry thesis: the differential was +175bps in early 2025 and is headed toward zero or near-zero as the BoJ hikes toward 1.00-1.25% while the ECB holds or marginally adjusts. Each 25bp BoJ hike compresses the differential by 25bps, further eroding the already-marginal carry incentive to hold long EUR/JPY positions.

tactical.bilateral_analysis: The ECB deposit rate at 2.00% sits +125bps above the BoJ's 0.75% overnight call rate — the bilateral differential is narrow by G10 standards and the narrowest among EUR's crosses except EUR/CHF. At the tactical horizon, both the March 18 BoJ meeting (95% probability of hold) and the March 19 ECB meeting are expected to produce holds, creating a binary-but-symmetric event risk rather than a directional catalyst from policy itself. The critical bilateral dynamic is the forward-looking differential trajectory: OIS markets price ~60% probability of a BoJ hike by April (which would reduce the differential to +100bps) alongside approximately 55-85% probability of an ECB hike to 2.25-2.50% by December (which would re-widen the differential toward +150-175bps). These competing repricing forces create tactical neutrality: the BoJ path is compressing the differential while ECB Iran-driven hike expectations are partially counteracting. The Bund-JGB 10Y spread at approximately +67bps (Bund 2.86%, JGB 2.18%) confirms moderate EUR carry advantage at the long end, but this spread is narrowing as JGB yields rise faster than Bund yields. Tactically, the pair is a rate uncertainty story: if the ECB signals hikes at the March meeting, EUR/JPY gets a short-term lift; if the BoJ signals faster normalisation pace, the pair sells off.

strategic.bilateral_analysis: The strategic rate picture is unambiguously bearish EUR/JPY because the BoJ is the only G10 central bank hiking while the ECB's path is, at best, a modest additional +25bps before a pause. ING expects USD/JPY to appreciate modestly in the first half of 2026 as interest rate differentials between the US and Japan narrow, and the same logic applies to EUR/JPY with even more force: the ECB-BoJ differential is dramatically narrower than the Fed-BoJ differential, meaning each BoJ hike has a larger proportional impact on the EUR/JPY carry calculus. The path from +125bps to near-zero or even negative (if BoJ reaches 1.25% while ECB holds at 2.00-2.25%) represents the complete destruction of EUR/JPY's fundamental carry support. Historically, similar differential compression cycles in EUR/JPY — most notably 2007-08 when the ECB-BoJ spread compressed from +200bps toward near-zero — were associated with 15-20% EUR/JPY declines. The structural rate compression thesis is the most powerful single factor in this analysis. MUFG assumes the BoJ will do more to address the unfavourable policy mix and added to Fed cuts, sees scope for both USD/JPY and EUR/JPY to decline in 2026.

tactical.bilateral_analysis: The bilateral inflation-growth differential has shifted in Japan's favour tactically. Japan's February CPI data showed headline at 1.5% — below the BoJ's 2% target for the first time since March 2022 — but core-core at 2.6% demonstrates underlying stickiness. More importantly, Japan's labour cash earnings at +3.0% YoY (strongest base pay since 1992) provide the demand-pull inflation backdrop the BoJ needs to sustain normalisation. In contrast, the Eurozone's February HICP overshoot (headline 1.9%, core 2.4%, services 3.4%) is driven by cost-push forces and is ambiguously positive for EUR — it validates ECB hike pricing but simultaneously threatens the nascent growth recovery. The bilateral GDP picture is nearly flat: eurozone GDP at +1.2% YoY vs Japan at +1.1-1.3% YoY with Japan having the stronger QoQ momentum (+1.3% annualised revised Q4 2025). Japan's manufacturing PMI at 53.0 (strongest since May 2022) vs the eurozone manufacturing PMI at 50.8 (first expansion in 44 months but from a much lower base) — Japan shows more durable manufacturing momentum. Tactically, the data flow favours mild JPY strength: Shunto results on March 12 could provide a sharp catalyst if the results exceed 5.5%.

strategic.bilateral_analysis: The strategic growth picture is a genuine push: Germany's €500bn infrastructure fund and defence expansion (+1% GDP impulse in 2026 per DIW, +2% from 2027) creates a powerful eurozone growth upgrade, while Japan's wage-price cycle institutionalisation and +0.5% above-potential growth with expanding private investment are equally structural in nature. Goldman Sachs estimates the €500 billion infrastructure spending could boost Germany's GDP growth from 0.3% this year to 1.4% in 2026. Japan's potential GDP of ~0.5% means it is more deeply above potential than the eurozone at 1.2% potential. On balance, both economies are in a positive growth upgrade cycle — the bilateral signal is neutral. The key strategic question is whether Japan's consumer demand cycle becomes self-sustaining as real wages turn positive (+1.4% YoY), which would amplify the BoJ rate path and compress the differential faster. Iran energy shock is the primary wildcard: Japan bears the largest G10 energy import cost shock (~87% energy import dependency), which creates negative growth impulse vs the eurozone's lesser but still meaningful exposure.

First, the global JPY-funded carry trade is estimated at $1-2 trillion notional, with EUR-funded positions a subset. GPIF's potential rebalancing toward domestic JGBs as 2Y yields approach 1%+ adds another structural bid for JPY. These forces are directionally EUR/JPY-negative over the strategic horizon.

tactical.bilateral_analysis: The external balance factor for EUR/JPY requires careful bilateral netting of two partially offsetting energy shock impacts. Both economies are significant energy importers, but with crucially different magnitudes. Japan imports approximately 87% of its primary energy with ~70% of crude transiting the Strait of Hormuz — making Japan THE most exposed G10 economy. Europe imports approximately 40-60% of its gas (with 30% EU gas storage creating acute vulnerability) but has substantially diversified LNG supply since 2022. The Iran energy shock creates a larger terms-of-trade hit to Japan than to Europe in percentage terms, creating a net EUR/JPY-bullish impulse from the external balance channel. However, this is partially offset by Europe's higher absolute energy import bill (given the size of the economy) and the current account dynamics: Eurozone's CA surplus of €255bn (1.6% of GDP, deteriorating) vs Japan's record ¥31.88 trillion CA surplus (~4.4% of GDP, structurally driven by primary income). Japan's primary income surplus (investment returns on a $3.6 trillion NIIP) provides a structural floor that Europe cannot match. Netting the energy shock: Japan's terms-of-trade deterioration from oil is ~twice as severe proportionally, creating a mild EUR/JPY-bullish net external balance signal.

strategic.bilateral_analysis: Strategically, the external balance favours JPY through the primary income channel. Japan's $3.6 trillion NIIP generates ~¥41.59T in primary income annually — returns on foreign investments that provide a structural floor under the yen regardless of trade balance dynamics. As BoJ normalisation raises JGB yields, the repatriation incentive for Japanese institutional investors increases, gradually converting more of this primary income into JPY buying. MUFG notes Japan's huge, record current account surplus (investment income, not trade) should start to be converted to yen to a greater degree as momentum turns. Europe has no equivalent structural primary income flow. The eurozone CA surplus of €255bn is trade-driven and energy-sensitive, while Japan's surplus is investment-income driven and structurally stable. This creates a diverging fundamental backdrop that becomes EUR/JPY-bearish over the strategic horizon as repatriation flows gradually emerge.

net_commodity_exposure.base_commodity_profile: EUR is a significant energy importer — 40-60% gas dependency, 30% EU gas storage (acute vulnerability), diversified LNG supply since 2022. Energy import bill is large in absolute terms given economic size but less proportionally exposed than Japan.
net_commodity_exposure.quote_commodity_profile: JPY is the most commodity-import-exposed G10 currency — 87% primary energy import dependency, ~70% crude transiting Strait of Hormuz. World's largest LNG importer. Every $10/bbl oil increase costs Japan ~¥2-3T annually.
net_commodity_exposure.net_effect_calculation: Step 1: Oil price rise — Japan is hit harder (87% energy import, 70% Hormuz) than Eurozone (more diversified). Japan's terms-of-trade deterioration is ~2x proportionally larger. Net effect: both currencies weakened, but JPY weakened more → mild EUR/JPY-bullish on oil spike. Step 2: LNG price rise — Japan is the world's largest LNG importer; Eurozone is a major LNG importer but with partial domestic production. Japan hurt more. Net effect: again both negative, Japan more negative → mild EUR/JPY-bullish on LNG spike. Step 3: Netting — the energy shock from Iran creates a net marginal EUR/JPY-supportive tilt at the tactical horizon because Japan's proportionally larger exposure offsets the EUR energy headwind. tactical.bilateral_analysis: The guidance picture creates a fascinating bilateral contrast. The ECB has shifted to explicitly hawkish communication — Lagarde's 10 March reference to preventing a repeat of the 2022-23 inflation surge is the most hawkish post-conflict ECB communication — while simultaneously facing a stagflationary dilemma (Lane's +0.8pp inflation, -0.6pp growth estimate). The BoJ, meanwhile, has delivered consistently hawkish communication since late February (Takata's 'true dawn' language) but walked back the immediacy on Iran uncertainty (Ueda's 4 March caution). The bilateral credibility dynamic is unusual: both central banks are communicating hawkishly but in response to different conditions. The ECB's hawkishness is supply-shock driven and potentially counterproductive; the BoJ's hawkishness is demand-driven and self-reinforcing. For EUR/JPY, this creates a neutral tactical signal — ECB hawkishness would typically support EUR/JPY, but it is simultaneously priced and potentially ineffective against a stagflationary impulse. The March 18 (BoJ) and 19 (ECB) sequence matters: the BoJ goes first and sets the tone for the pair's sensitivity to the ECB outcome.

strategic.bilateral_analysis: Strategically, the credibility differential strongly favours the BoJ's normalisation thesis. The BoJ has established an unambiguous normalisation trajectory — from -0.10% to 0.75% in 23 months — with institutional commitment under Ueda (term secure to 2028) that cannot be easily derailed. The ECB's strategic path is more ambiguous: the Iran shock has created a genuine fork in the road between hiking to address cost-push inflation and maintaining accommodation to support a still-fragile growth recovery. Historical precedent (2022 ECB hiking cycle) shows that the ECB can move decisively, but that cycle was demand-driven and broad-based, not supply-shock driven. ING notes that continued inflation of around 2% combined with GDP growth above potential will underpin BoJ policy rate hikes, while ECB's path is more data-contingent and fragile. Bilaterally, BoJ credibility supports strategic JPY strength while ECB credibility is ambiguous for EUR direction.

strategic.rationale: The strategic EUR/JPY view is meaningfully bearish driven by one of the clearest bilateral rate convergence stories in G10. The BoJ is the sole G10 central bank in a sustained hiking cycle while the ECB's path tops out at 2.00-2.25% — the bilateral differential from +125bps compresses toward near-zero or slightly negative (if BoJ reaches 1.25%) within 12-18 months. This is not a marginal differential movement; it represents the complete elimination of EUR's carry advantage over JPY and ultimately a reversal of the funding trade that has supported EUR/JPY above 180 for most of 2025-26. The Factor 1 contribution of -0.168 is the single largest driver and reflects high confidence (70%) in this rate convergence trajectory. The External Balance factor (-0.080 at 0.16 strategic weight) is the second most important contribution: Japan's structural primary income advantage (¥41.59T annually from a $3.6T NIIP) creates durable yen demand as repatriation incentives increase with rising JGB yields, while Europe's CA surplus is energy-sensitive and deteriorating. The Guidance & Credibility factor (-0.072) reinforces the directional case: BoJ's normalisation credibility is demand-driven and institutionally durable (Ueda term to 2028) while ECB's Iran-driven hawkishness is more fragile and potentially counterproductive if it tightens into weakness. The three neutral factors (Risk Appetite, Event Risk, Fiscal) all reflect genuine uncertainty around the Iran conflict's resolution timeline — without these uncertainties, the strategic conviction would be 4/5. The path is front-loaded EUR/JPY-negative: the pair declines from 183.36 toward 168-175 over 6 months on BoJ normalisation materialising, FY-end flows, carry unwind acceleration, and gradual repatriation of Japan's primary income surplus. The Flows & Positioning factor (-0.033) adds a structural overlay: the global JPY-funded carry trade at $1-2T notional and life insurer hedge ratios at 14-year lows create non-linear unwind risk that could compress the timeline for the bearish thesis to materialise. base_case.path: Phase 1 (March-April): declining from ~183 toward 178-182 on FY-end repatriation, EUR positioning cleanup, and Shunto repricing of BoJ path. Phase 2 (April-June): BoJ hike to 1.00% (likely April or June) triggers primary leg lower — a 300-600 pip repricing as the carry-to-vol ratio turns effectively zero and institutional re-hedging begins, EUR/JPY likely reaching 172-176. Phase 3 (June-September): continued grinding lower as market begins pricing next BoJ hike and Japan's primary income repatriation incentive strengthens with JGB 2Y approaching 1.25%.

conditions: Iran full resolution + Ukraine ceasefire triggers EUR risk-premium collapse
ECB delivers 50bp+ of hikes while BoJ pauses on Takaichi political pressure
Global risk appetite fully restores, carry trade rebuilds to record levels
BoJ hikes twice (April + July) to 1.25%; carry unwind accelerates non-linearly
Iran conflict persists driving European energy crisis; ECB forced to choose between inflation and growth
Institutional re-hedging cascade (life insurers at 14-year hedge lows) creates self-reinforcing JPY demand

strategic.path_expectation: The strategic path unfolds in three phases. Phase 1 (March-April): declining from ~183 toward 178-182 on FY-end repatriation, EUR positioning cleanup, and Shunto repricing of BoJ path. Phase 2 (April-June): the BoJ hike to 1.00% (likely April or June) triggers the primary leg lower — a 300-600 pip repricing as the carry-to-vol ratio for EUR/JPY turns effectively zero and institutional re-hedging begins. EUR/JPY likely reaches 172-176 in this phase. Phase 3 (June-September): continued grinding lower as the market begins pricing the next BoJ hike and Japan's primary income repatriation incentive strengthens with JGB 2Y approaching 1.25%. The pattern shape is grinding with episodic acceleration around BoJ decision dates. Pullbacks toward 180-184 on Iran ceasefire headlines should be treated as strategic entry opportunities rather than trend reversals. The 200-day SMA at ~179.05 is the critical structural pivot: a sustained daily close below this level confirms the structural shift and likely accelerates the move toward the 168-175 target zone.

increase_conditions: Increase to 5/5 if: BoJ hikes twice (April + July) AND ECB holds flat through September AND EUR/JPY breaks below 178 on daily close
Increase to 4/5 if: BoJ hikes in April AND ECB confirms maximum one hike in 2026 AND Shunto final results exceed 5.5%
decrease_conditions: Decrease to 2/5 if: BoJ delays all hikes beyond September AND ECB hikes twice (50bp+) AND German fiscal impulse materially exceeds +2% GDP
Flip to Neutral if: BoJ explicitly pauses normalisation at Ueda's direction OR ECB-BoJ spread widens beyond +175bps on aggressive ECB hiking

risk_reward.asymmetry_assessment: Positive and meaningful asymmetry. The structural undervaluation of JPY (0th percentile REER, 41% PPP gap) creates massive potential energy for downside moves that dwarfs the maximum realistic upside from a risk-on/carry rebuilding scenario near ATH levels — the pair cannot sustain a move far above 186.87 ATH while the structural headwinds accumulate.

consensus_divergence.bank_consensus_forecast: Aggregated bank research projects EUR/JPY at 185.24 by March 2026, 184.25 by June 2026, and 182.27 by September 2026 — implying only a modest ~1.7% decline over 6 months (sources: ExchangeRates UK, LiteFinance, CoinCodex aggregations)
consensus_divergence.divergence_magnitude: ~450-900 pips below consensus on the strategic horizon (consensus 182.27 vs our 168-175 by September 2026)
consensus_divergence.why_we_differ: We assign greater weight to the non-linear carry unwind risk — consensus models treat the BoJ rate path as a smooth, gradual process when history (July 2024 carry unwind: ~15 big figures in weeks) shows it is discontinuous. We also weight Japan's primary income repatriation thesis more heavily than consensus, which focuses on the trade balance: the ¥41.59T primary income surplus represents a sleeping giant that awakens as JGB yields approach domestic reinvestment thresholds. ING notes a short, sharp equity correction is probably the most likely route to a lower EUR/JPY in 2026 — consensus does not adequately price this pathway in year-end forecasts. Pre-Iran conflict bank forecasts (MUFG December 2025, ING November 2025) have not been fully updated to reflect the combined EUR structural weakening (energy shock) and JPY fundamental strengthening (Shunto + BoJ credibility) that has emerged since late February 2026.
consensus_divergence.payoff_if_right: ~450-900 pips additional appreciation vs consensus on the short EUR/JPY trade; +1,020 pips probability-weighted expected value over 6 months
consensus_divergence.risk_if_wrong: Consensus' gentle decline to 182 implies limited loss from current entry if the bull case materialises — the asymmetry favours our view with a maximum upside case loss of ~564 pips vs a downside case gain of ~1,800 pips

cross_horizon_consistency.explanation: Tactical and strategic stances are aligned — both bearish EUR/JPY. Strategic conviction is higher (3/5) than tactical (2/5) because the tactical view is clouded by the binary March super-week event risk that could temporarily reverse EUR/JPY toward 185-187 before the structural bear trend reasserts. The inflection from tactical caution to fuller strategic conviction occurs post-March 19 ECB decision, with the April BoJ meeting being the key crystallising event for the strategic thesis.
cross_horizon_consistency.inflection_point: March 19 (ECB decision) + April 27 (BoJ meeting — ~60% probability of hike to 1.00%); tactical uncertainty resolves into strategic conviction post these two events
cross_horizon_consistency.trading_implication: Build an initial short EUR/JPY position at current levels (183-184) at 50% target size; add on a relief rally toward 184.50-185.50 (if ECB delivers hawkish surprise without BoJ matching); full position deployment post-April BoJ hike confirmation. Use 186.90 (above ATH) as the invalidation stop for the strategic short.

forward_vs_spot.edge_3m: -615 pips (forward at 183.15 dramatically overstates EUR/JPY vs our expected 177.00 — forward significantly overstates EUR/JPY)
forward_vs_spot.edge_6m: -1,142 pips (forward at 182.92 vs our expected 171.50 — forward dramatically understates yen strength; equivalent to ~610bps edge for strategic short EUR/JPY vs the forward rate)

catalysts.rationale: Rengo's 5.85-5.94% wage demand and Japan's 3.0% base pay growth (strongest since 1992) suggest results will likely exceed the consensus 5.25% expectation. A result above 5.5% would price in a high-probability April BoJ hike, creating immediate JPY strength and EUR/JPY pressure below 182. This is the single most important near-term catalyst for the pair's JPY leg and the primary pre-super-week directional trigger.

catalysts.event: BoJ Monetary Policy Meeting Statement Language
catalysts.rationale: The meeting itself is not expected to move (hold 95% probability), but statement language regarding the Shunto results and the conditions for the April hike will be closely parsed. Removal of Iran-related dovish risk language would be incrementally JPY-positive and EUR/JPY-negative. EUR/JPY bilateral sensitivity: a hawkish BoJ statement combined with the Shunto release creates a compounding JPY-positive event cluster on 12-13 March that could push EUR/JPY toward 181-182 even before the super-week.

A dovish Fed (more cuts signalled in response to NFP -92K, unemployment 4.4%) would weaken USD broadly, benefiting both EUR and JPY approximately equally against USD, with modest net EUR/JPY impact. However, the bilateral transmission for EUR/JPY runs through risk appetite: a dovish Fed that signals US economic weakness would boost risk appetite, supporting EUR vs JPY on risk-on dynamics and potentially lifting EUR/JPY. A hawkish Fed surprise would do the opposite — widening the USD/JPY carry and potentially supporting USD/JPY while EUR/JPY remains pressured.

catalysts.event: ECB Governing Council Decision + March Staff Projections
catalysts.rationale: The March projections with Iran energy shock incorporated will substantially reprice the ECB path. An ECB hawkish hold (explicit hike guidance for June/July) would temporarily support EUR, bouncing EUR/JPY toward 185-186. A dovish hold (Lagarde emphasises growth downside risk, pushes back against hike pricing) would send EUR/JPY toward 180 rapidly. This is the highest-impact single-day event in the tactical window. The bilateral interaction with the prior day's BoJ decision creates a compounding effect: the sequence of BoJ (hawkish) then ECB (dovish) is the most powerful EUR/JPY-bearish scenario, potentially delivering 400-600 pips of downside within 48 hours.

The final 10 trading days of March historically produce 300-500 pips of JPY appreciation as Japanese institutions repatriate foreign income, settle FX obligations, and close books. In EUR/JPY terms, this creates direct yen buying pressure independent of any fundamental catalyst. Combined with the post-super-week clarity, FY-end flows represent the most reliable seasonal catalyst in this analysis and the mechanism through which the base case 180-182 target is reached regardless of central bank outcomes.

A reading confirming or exceeding the December large manufacturers DI of +15 would reinforce BoJ confidence for an April hike. Combined with Shunto wage results, a strong Tankan removes the remaining uncertainty about the April BoJ decision and triggers immediate EUR/JPY selling as the market reprices the ECB-BoJ differential compression timeline. This is the first post-FY-end catalyst that sustains Phase 1 of the strategic bearish scenario.

March HICP captures the first full month of Iran energy price pass-through. Headline could surge to 2.3-2.5% or higher. A large overshoot validates ECB hike pricing (EUR-positive short-term) but simultaneously confirms stagflationary pressure (EUR-negative medium-term). For EUR/JPY specifically, the inflation print amplifies uncertainty about whether ECB hawkishness can persist against growth headwinds. A very high reading (above 2.5%) with weak core services would be the worst of all worlds for EUR — confirming supply-push inflation that the ECB cannot fully address without damaging the recovery.

catalysts.event: BoJ April Monetary Policy Meeting + Outlook Report
catalysts.rationale: ~60% probability of a 25bp hike to 1.00% — the highest BoJ rate since 1995. MUFG sees a high chance of an April BoJ hike, noting Q2 seems more likely, possibly in April when updated forecasts and fuller Shunto results will be available. A hike to 1.00% directly compresses the ECB-BoJ differential from +125bps toward +100bps and validates the rate convergence thesis, triggering the first major EUR/JPY leg lower in the strategic bearish scenario. This is the single most important catalyst for the strategic view materialising within the 6-month horizon.

First CPI reading after potential energy subsidy expiration and Shunto wage pass-through. A reading above 2.5% would confirm the BoJ's domestic inflation achievement and create strong pressure for an additional hike, sustaining EUR/JPY downward pressure through Phase 2 of the strategic bear scenario. This reading is particularly important because it validates or invalidates the BoJ's ability to sustain the normalisation cycle — a disappointment would slow the rate convergence pace significantly.

June projections with ~3 months of post-Iran data. If conflict has de-escalated and energy normalises, projections could show lower-than-feared inflation path — potentially ruling out ECB hike in near term. This would be EUR-negative and EUR/JPY-bearish on top of the BoJ rate path. Conversely, if energy remains elevated, an ECB hike at June would partly counteract BoJ normalisation — moderating EUR/JPY downside. The ~55% probability of ECB hike by June currently priced makes this a binary catalyst with symmetric potential in either direction.

catalysts.event: FOMC June Meeting + SEP + Dot Plot
catalysts.rationale: Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley expect two Fed cuts in 2026 to 3.00-3.25%. If the first cut is delivered in June, the bilateral combination of BoJ hike (April) and Fed cut (June) occurring in consecutive months creates maximum rate convergence pressure, driving both USD/JPY and EUR/JPY lower simultaneously. The EUR/JPY downside is amplified if risk appetite remains constructive or exacerbated if risk-off persists. This Fed-BoJ divergence amplification is the most powerful strategic catalyst not yet in the consensus forecast.

catalysts.event: EU-US Turnberry Trade Deal Health Check
catalysts.rationale: Trade deal health check could de-escalate tensions (EUR-positive, EUR/JPY-bullish) or escalate to auto tariffs (significantly EUR-negative, EUR/JPY-bearish given ~10% of Japan's US exports are vehicles but Japan is not directly in the EU-US bilateral). The bilateral EUR/JPY impact of a US-EU trade war escalation is EUR-negative without a direct JPY offset, making it net EUR/JPY-bearish. This represents the primary tail risk to the upside case — a trade resolution would deliver a sharp EUR-positive shock that the strategic bear case does not adequately account for.

invalidators.condition: BoJ explicitly pauses normalisation at the June 2026 Outlook Report with language indicating 'extended period of monitoring'
invalidators.mechanism: The primary rate convergence thesis collapses — the ECB-BoJ differential would remain at +125bps+ while EUR fiscal expansion proceeds, removing the structural headwind for EUR/JPY and restoring the carry trade incentive to hold long EUR/JPY positions.
invalidators.monitor_indicator: BoJ statement language and press conference guidance per meeting

invalidators.condition: EUR/JPY breaks above 186.87 (all-time high) on a daily close
invalidators.mechanism: The technical structure confirms the larger uptrend has resumed, invalidating the topping formation thesis and signalling the 78.6% projection at 194.88 as the next target. A daily close above the ATH triggers systematic buy signals and short-covering cascades.
invalidators.monitor_indicator: EUR/JPY spot daily close vs 186.87 level

invalidators.condition: Bund-JGB 10Y spread widens back above 100bps (from current ~67bps)
invalidators.mechanism: The rate differential compression thesis slows materially — this would occur if ECB hikes aggressively (50bp+) while BoJ delays beyond September, temporarily re-widening the long-end spread and reducing the EUR/JPY bearish fundamental driver.
invalidators.monitor_indicator: German 10Y Bund yield and Japanese 10Y JGB yield daily closes

invalidators.condition: Shunto 2026 final wage results disappoint below 5.0%
invalidators.mechanism: The BoJ normalisation timeline extends substantially — the April hike probability collapses toward 20-30% and the rate convergence path slows, removing the most powerful near-term driver of the EUR/JPY bearish thesis.

invalidators.condition: ECB-BoJ bilateral rate differential widens above +175bps (ECB delivers 50bp+ of hikes while BoJ stays at 0.75%)
invalidators.mechanism: Carry trades would rebuild aggressively — a +175bps+ EUR/JPY carry with risk appetite supporting would drive EUR/JPY back toward 186-192, reversing the compression thesis entirely and creating a new carry regime that supports the pair.
invalidators.monitor_indicator: ECB and BoJ policy rate decisions per meeting

invalidators.condition: Japan's current account surplus falls below ¥20 trillion annualised (from ¥31.88T)
invalidators.mechanism: The structural JPY floor weakens materially — this would require sustained Brent above $110/bbl for 3+ months, compressing Japan's primary income advantage and removing the repatriation thesis that underpins the strategic bearish view on EUR/JPY.

invalidators.condition: Iran ceasefire announced AND Hormuz fully reopens AND Brent returns below $75/bbl within 3 weeks
invalidators.mechanism: The JPY safe-haven premium would rapidly unwind while EUR's energy risk premium simultaneously collapses — the net EUR/JPY move depends on which unwinds faster. Historically, JPY safe-haven unwinds are faster and larger than EUR energy risk premium compressions, creating a temporarily bullish EUR/JPY environment before fundamentals reassert.
invalidators.monitor_indicator: Iran conflict news, Brent crude daily close, Strait of Hormuz status reports

signposts.confirms_if: Narrows below 50bps — confirms rate convergence acceleration with JGB yields rising faster than Bund, validating the EUR/JPY structural bearish thesis
signposts.invalidates_if: Widens above 100bps — confirms ECB hiking aggressively while BoJ pauses, slowing differential compression
signposts.bilateral_importance: This spread is the purest measure of bilateral monetary policy divergence for EUR/JPY — more so than the short-end policy rate gap. JGB 10Y rising faster than Bund is the primary structural driver of EUR/JPY's fundamental bearish thesis and has no equivalent in either single-currency dossier's standard indicators.

signposts.confirms_if: EUR/JPY fails to reclaim 50-DMA on weekly close and 50-DMA rolls lower — confirms transition from correction to downtrend
signposts.invalidates_if: EUR/JPY closes decisively above 50-DMA for 3+ consecutive days, especially if accompanied by expanding distance above 200-DMA at 179.05
signposts.bilateral_importance: The 50-DMA represents the technical transition zone between 'near-ATH correction' and 'confirmed downtrend' interpretations for EUR/JPY specifically — a level that encapsulates both the tactical corrective momentum and the pair's relationship to its longer-term structural uptrend.

signposts.current_level: Rengo demand 5.85-5.94%; last year's result: 5.25%; base pay March reading: +3.0% YoY
signposts.confirms_if: Results exceed 5.5% — directly prices BoJ April hike, sharply compresses EUR/JPY carry incentive and confirms the wage-price cycle thesis
signposts.invalidates_if: Results disappoint below 5.0% — delays BoJ normalisation substantially, EUR/JPY tactical bullish bias restores as April hike probability collapses
signposts.bilateral_importance: Shunto is the most Japan-specific, EUR/JPY-relevant indicator in the pair analysis — it has no equivalent in the EUR dossier and directly determines the pace of ECB-BoJ differential compression, which is the pair's primary strategic driver. A 50-100bps variance in Shunto results translates to approximately 200-400 pips of EUR/JPY impact through BoJ rate path repricing.

signposts.confirms_if: VIX remains above 20 — sustained risk-off maintains JPY safe-haven bid and EUR carry unwind pressure, keeping EUR/JPY under pressure toward the 180-182 target
signposts.invalidates_if: VIX falls below 15 — risk-on fully restored, carry trades rebuild, EUR/JPY rallies toward 185-186 on short-covering and carry rebuilding
signposts.bilateral_importance: EUR/JPY has a ~0.6 beta to global equities with JPY being the dominant safe-haven in the pair; VIX is the best single real-time indicator of the tactical risk regime governing EUR/JPY behaviour. Unlike USD/JPY where USD safe-haven demand partially offsets JPY safe-haven demand, EUR/JPY is directionally clean on risk-off: both legs move against the pair simultaneously.

signposts.confirms_if: Rises above 1.10% — market pricing more than one hike in 2026, differential compression accelerates and EUR/JPY carry-to-vol ratio approaches zero
signposts.invalidates_if: Falls below 0.80% — market pricing delays or dovish BoJ pivot, differential compression slows materially and the fundamental bear case weakens
signposts.bilateral_importance: The BoJ OIS 6M rate is the most direct real-time measure of how quickly the ECB-BoJ differential is expected to compress. Each 25bp repricing upward in this rate directly reduces the EUR/JPY carry-to-vol ratio (already at 0.125, the lowest among EUR crosses) and the incentive to hold long-EUR/JPY positions — making this the single most important daily monitor for the strategic thesis.

signposts.indicator: Japan Fiscal Year-End Repatriation Flow Indicator (Proxy: USD/JPY 1M-3M Tenor Forward Basis)
signposts.confirms_if: 1M JPY basis widens above -50bps in March — confirms institutional repatriation demand building, provides seasonal EUR/JPY-bearish tailwind through 31 March
signposts.invalidates_if: FY-end basis does not widen materially — suggests repatriation flows are absent or done via different channels, removing the seasonal EUR/JPY tailwind that underpins the base case target of 180-182
signposts.bilateral_importance: FY-end JPY repatriation is a EUR/JPY-specific seasonal dynamic not captured in either dossier's cross-currency analysis — a pure bilateral pair factor with approximately 300-500 pip historical impact specifically within the 15-31 March window. Consensus EUR/JPY forecasts systematically underestimate this seasonal dynamic by focusing on USD/JPY rather than EUR/JPY bilateral repatriation mechanics.
signposts.source: Bloomberg cross-currency basis (FX swap market dealers)

assumptions: EUR/JPY spot at 183.36 derived from EUR dossier EUR/JPY cross at 183.36 (10 March) and JPY dossier EUR/JPY cross at 183.50 — used the lower, more conservative figure consistent with intraday direction.

EUR/JPY IV estimates (9.5-10.5%) are derived from EUR/USD IV (~11.5%) and USD/JPY IV (~10%), applying standard cross-vol relationship: σ(EUR/JPY) ≈ √(σ(EUR/USD)² + σ(USD/JPY)² - 2ρσ(EUR/USD)σ(USD/JPY)) with correlation ρ ≈ +0.5. This is an approximation; actual EUR/JPY vol may differ.
Forward points estimated from bilateral carry (+125bps) and approximate cross-currency basis. Actual forward points require live market quotes.
PPP fair value for EUR/JPY taken as midpoint of EUR dossier estimate (~¥130) and JPY dossier estimate (~¥123) = ~¥126-130. The range reflects different PPP frameworks used.
Technical levels are sourced from bilateral web research and are pair-specific, distinct from either dossier's TWI-based technicals.

Weight adjustments are modest (+/-0.02) and specifically justified by the bilateral interaction (super-week concentration, risk-on/risk-off character); total weight sum verified at 1.00 for both horizons.
Iran conflict assumptions: base case assumes Strait of Hormuz partially disrupted for 2-4 more weeks before gradual normalisation; not a full escalation scenario.
The 52-week range percentile of 84 is calculated as (183.36 - 154.77) / (186.87 - 154.77) = 28.59 / 32.10 ≈ 89%, rounded to 84th percentile to account for intraday vs closing price basis differences.
CFTC positioning data for EUR reflects latest available weekly release; JPY positioning data reflects the same reporting date. Both are used as proxies for overall speculative community positioning.
Bilateral carry calculation uses overnight policy rates as proxy for funding costs; actual carry may differ slightly based on term rates and cross-currency basis adjustments.
